content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all five pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation durability. Amendments 1+4 (same root cause): After verify_and_comment_mappings returns, verified mappings are now persisted back to both journal and NDJSON via record_planned_elsewhere_mapping with mapped_bead_verified=true, using the append-only design where the new record supersedes the original unverified one; the existing filter on !m.mapped_bead_verified prevents re-processing on replay. Amendment 2: Changed the force-completed early return condition in final_review.rs from !final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() to final_accepted_amendments.iter().any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none()), so force-completion only triggers when there are actual non-planned-elsewhere amendments that would require a restart. Amendment 3: Added br_mutation.sync_flush() call in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success after verify_and_comment_mappings completes, gated on whether any comments were posted, ensuring br comment mutations are durable before the function returns. Amendment 5: Modified load_planned_elsewhere_mappings to fall back to rebuilding from journal PlannedElsewhereMapped events when the NDJSON sidecar is empty or missing, via new rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal function that extracts mapping fields from journal event metadata.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendments 1+4: In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs), after verify_and_comment_mappings returns outcomes, iterate over verified outcomes and persist each as a new PlannedElsewhereMapping with mapped_bead_verified=true via record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (writes to both journal and NDJSON). Added PlannedElsewhereMapping to the model import.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: In final_review.rs, changed the force-completed early return condition from !final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() to final_accepted_amendments.iter().any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none()), so the force-completion path only activates when non-planned-elsewhere amendments exist that would require a restart.
3. [Completed] Amendment 3: In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs), added br_mutation.sync_flush() call after verify_and_comment_mappings completes, gated on any_comments flag. Failures are logged as warnings but do not block reconciliation.
4. [Completed] Amendment 5: Modified load_planned_elsewhere_mappings in service.rs to check if NDJSON read returns empty results; when empty, falls back to rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal which reads all journal events, filters for PlannedElsewhereMapped type, and reconstructs PlannedElsewhereMapping structs from event metadata (active_bead_id, mapped_to_bead_id, mapped_bead_verified) and details (finding_summary).
5. [Completed] Run cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo fmt (applied one minor indent fix), cargo fmt --check (clean), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (lib, integration, unit, doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues (one auto-fix applied during development for indent in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal)
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (success_reconciliation.rs, final_review.rs, service.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing
- Amendment 1+4 verified: verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success now calls record_planned_elsewhere_mapping for each verified outcome with mapped_bead_verified=true, writing to both journal (audit) and NDJSON (queryable index); the unverified filter at line 701 prevents re-processing on subsequent runs
- Amendment 2 verified: force-completed early return now uses has_non_pe_for_force_check = final_accepted_amendments.iter().any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none()) instead of !final_accepted_amendments.is_empty()
- Amendment 3 verified: sync_flush is called after verify_and_comment_mappings when any_comments is true, ensuring br comment mutations are durable; failure is logged as warning and does not block reconciliation
- Amendment 5 verified: load_planned_elsewhere_mappings falls back to rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal when NDJSON returns empty; the rebuild function reads journal events, filters for PlannedElsewhereMapped, and reconstructs mappings from metadata fields
- Files modified: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (persist verified mappings + sync_flush), src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs (force-complete guard), src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (journal fallback)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_creates_finds_and_resets_checkpoint_commits) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change
- The journal fallback in load_planned_elsewhere_mappings reads the full journal to rebuild mappings, which could be slow for very large journals; acceptable since it only triggers when the NDJSON sidecar is missing/empty (a rare recovery scenario)
- Post-run verification persistence is best-effort: if the verified mapping write fails (journal or NDJSON), the warning is logged but reconciliation proceeds; the next run will re-verify (safe but redundant)
